factors.name: Tech selloff momentum
factors.summary: Nasdaq saw largest single-day point drop on record Friday (>1,121 points) with $1.8T S&P 500 wipeout. 4h change -1.47%, 24h change -2.73% indicates ongoing selling pressure.

factors.name: Strong US jobs data
factors.summary: Robust employment data fueling expectations Fed will keep rates higher for longer, pressuring long-duration growth multiples and AI-heavy mega-cap valuations.

factors.name: Real yields / rate expectations
factors.summary: Bond traders betting on biggest CPI surge in years this week (per Bloomberg). Higher nominal rates + inflation expectations compress tech multiples; no dovish Fed pivot priced in.

factors.name: Middle East escalation (geopolitical)
factors.summary: Iran fired missiles at Israel over weekend, fragile ceasefire threatened despite Trump negotiations attempt. Oil surged >$2; risk-off sentiment driving equity rotation away from tech.

factors.name: Sector rotation underway
factors.summary: Investors dumping tech (NVDA, AAPL, MSFT hits) and rotating into health insurers, banks, retailers per MarketWatch. Mega-cap AI/cloud names under pressure; capex concerns rising.

factors.name: Hyperscaler capex flood looming
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports wave of new mega-cap equity issuance coming to fund AI capex (AWS, Azure, GCP guidance awaited). Questions on buyer depth and dilution pressure mounting.

factors.name: NVDA-TSMC semiconductor partnership news
factors.summary: NVDA and SK hynix announce multiyear memory deal (positive for chip demand), but drowned out by broader tech selloff and rate concerns. Constructive but insufficient to counter macro headwinds.

factors.name: Japan GDP beat, yen strength risk
factors.summary: Japan Q1 GDP 1.8% q/q annualized vs 1.3% expected; supportive of yen. USD/JPY above 160 may trigger intervention. Yen strength = headwind for NQ (31% of Nasdaq has international revenue).

factors.name: Dollar strength from risk-off
factors.summary: AUD/USD, NZD/USD, GBP/USD all softening Monday as Middle East tensions and stronger USD drive risk-off flows. Stronger dollar pressures international earnings for mega-cap tech exporters.

factors.name: Upcoming CPI data (this week)
factors.summary: US CPI expected this week; traders positioning for inflation surprise. Any hot print = further Fed hike expectations = extended tech multiple compression risk.

key_levels.resistance: 29,200 (intraday bounce target), 29,500 (upper end of pre-Friday range)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq100 is in PHASE 2 of a sharp tech-led selloff that began Friday with the largest single-day point drop on record. The catalyst mix is potent: strong US jobs data has eliminated any near-term Fed rate-cut expectations, real yields are rising ahead of this week's likely hot CPI print, and geopolitical risk (Iran missile attacks) has triggered classic risk-off rotation out of long-duration growth names into cyclicals and defensive sectors. Mega-cap tech valuations—particularly NVDA, AAPL, MSFT—were already stretched, and hyperscaler capex concerns are mounting as a flood of new equity issuance looms. While NVDA-TSMC partnership news is constructive for chip demand, it is overwhelmed by macro headwinds.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI (likely this week Jun 10–12 range) — hot print would validate bear case, keep rate expectations high
Apple WWDC 2026 (Jun 9) — AI/cloud strategy commentary could ease or worsen sentiment
BoC interest rate decision (early June) — dovish surprise could help risk-on, hawkish keeps pressure on growth
ECB decision (early June window) — same dynamic
FOMC minutes or Fed speakers — any hint of rate hold = some relief; hike signal = tech pressure persists
Mega-cap earnings commentary on capex / cloud guidance (pending MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN updates)
Treasury 10Y yield moves — >4.30% likely = further tech compression
screening.risk_warning: Move is already -2.73% in 24h (PHASE 2). Confidence capped at 6 because: (1) Asia markets open Monday may gap down further or bounce, creating noise; (2) Trump's public statements on Iran deal (appears he still wants negotiation) could reduce geopolitical premium, spurring relief rally; (3) if CPI comes in cooler than expected or Fed signals pause, bias flips bullish quickly; (4) mega-cap oversold bounce (NVDA, AAPL, MSFT gamma / short-covering) could reverse 200-500 point move intraday.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended in 4h timeframe. Avoid aggressive shorts here. Wait for retest of 29,000–29,200 resistance (likely intraday bounce zone) before fresh short entry. If long, enter only on break below 28,700 support with tight stops; stop-loss at 28,500. CPI data this week is the key event — hold bias light until then or until technical break + Fed confirmation.

factors.name: AI trade unwinding
factors.summary: South Korean Kospi crashed >8% at open (KOSPI circuit breaker triggered), with SK Hynix and Samsung collapsing as AI rally loses steam. Nasdaq itself down -2.73% in 24h following Friday's -4.2% rout on Broadcom weakness and AI capex skepticism.

factors.name: Fed rate hike repricing
factors.summary: Goldman Sachs dropped call for December 2026 rate cut; now expects hold until June 2027. Market is pricing in potential hike as early as September (not just hold). Strong May jobs data and persistent wage pressure support higher-for-longer narrative, compressing long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Real yields climbing
factors.summary: Treasuries sold off (10Y yields rising) amid Fed hike bets and geopolitical risk premium from Iran-Israel escalation. Higher real yields are particularly toxic for unprofitable high-growth tech names that dominate NDX.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk (Iran-Israel)
factors.summary: Iran launched coordinated ballistic missile attacks on Israel; Israeli military struck Iranian military targets and reported damage to Karoon Petrochemical complex. Strait of Hormuz transit threatened. Oil jumped 3%+ and equity futures swung volatile, signaling risk-off sentiment headwinds for growth equities.

factors.name: Tech-specific sentiment deterioration
factors.summary: Bank of America's Savita Subramanian flagged 'too many red flags' paralleling Feb 2020; energy outperforming, tech expensive valuations. Nvidia CEO's 'buying opportunity' framing couldn't stop Asia tech rout, suggesting conviction-of-conviction is fragile.

factors.name: Upcoming macro data risk
factors.summary: ECB poised to hike this week (economist warnings on 2011 repeat error). CPI, PCE data and any further hawkish surprise could deepen selloff. Market highly sensitive to any inflation re-confirmation.

factors.name: Dollar strength
factors.summary: USD near 100.10 (DXY steady), supported by geopolitical risk-off and Fed hike bets. Stronger dollar is a headwind for mega-cap international revenue (MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN overseas earnings), neutral-to-bearish for NDX.

key_levels.support: 28,600–28,700 (Friday's gap-down area); 28,300 (prior swing low); 27,500 (major structural support ~4 weeks ago)
key_levels.resistance: 29,144–29,150 (noted in intraday technical; buyers defended here early Monday); 29,500 (Friday's close ~29,550, pullback zone)

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in Phase 2 of a tech-driven correction triggered by Friday's -4.2% Broadcom/AI capex scare, now compounded by Fed rate hike repricing (Goldman Sachs extends hold, market prices September hike risk) and a sharp geopolitical escalation (Iran-Israel missile strikes threatening Strait of Hormuz and regional stability). Real yields are climbing on hawkish Fed bets and geopolitical risk premium; long-duration growth tech is most vulnerable. Asia tech contagion (Kospi down >8%, circuit breaker hit) signals broad-based AI trade unwind, not isolated weakness. While Nvidia CEO called it a 'buying opportunity,' sentiment fragility and emerging BofA red flags suggest more downside is likely before a durable bounce.
catalysts_ahead: ECB policy decision this week (Wednesday/Thursday expected) — hawkish surprise would deepen sell-off
CPI, Core CPI data (timing TBD in June window) — any inflation reacceleration likely BEARISH for NDX
PCE data — key Fed barometer; surprise on either side could trigger fresh re-pricing
Fed speakers early week (given hike repricing); any commentary on September tightening would be BEARISH
Nvidia earnings call Q&A on cloud capex demand and hyperscaler order trends — critical for sentiment reset
Ongoing Iran-Israel escalation updates (ceasefire risk, Strait of Hormuz closure scenarios)
screening.risk_warning: This bias is invalidated if: (1) ECB/Fed guidance turns dovish and real yields collapse >5-10 bps; (2) Iran-Israel conflict de-escalates rapidly with Trump-brokered deal (oil drops, risk-off unwinds); (3) major mega-cap earnings beats with raised guidance (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL next cycle) restore confidence in AI capex ROI; (4) circuit-breaker bounce in Asia/Korea stabilizes and reverses the contagion momentum; or (5) Nasdaq closes >29,300 with clean continuation above 29,144–29,150, invalidating Phase 2 setup.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already -2.73% in 24h and extended intraday; prefer to wait for retest of 29,144–29,150 support or 29,300 resistance before fresh short entry. If bounce stalls there, short risk-reward improves. Alternatively, on further break of 28,600, consider adds into structural support zone, but this is a 'lower for longer' corrective structure, not capitulation yet—watch for VIX spike and options gamma inversions as confirmation of capitulation.

factors.name: Tech selloff underway (Friday + Monday)
factors.summary: NAS100 down 2.73% in 24h; tech-led selloff Friday with NVDA, Broadcom losses continuing into Asia/Europe Monday. Semiconductor sector weakness (SK Hynix -8%, Kospi -8% open) signals demand/sentiment deterioration.

factors.name: NFP data (hawkish read, Friday release)
factors.summary: Hot job gains with upward revisions; unemployment fell to 4.29%. Markets fully priced Fed rate increase probability; gold sold off below $4,300 (lowest since March) as investors repriced inflation and Fed tightening odds.

factors.name: Real yields / nominal yields rising
factors.summary: Bond yields elevated on inflation fears post-NFP and Middle East escalation. Higher real yields compress long-duration growth multiples; tech/NDX extremely sensitive to real rate moves.

factors.name: Oil spike on Iran-Israel tensions
factors.summary: WTI jumped ~$4 to $92.50; Brent surged toward $96 after fresh missile strikes. Inflation concerns from higher oil prices reinforce hawkish Fed repricing and margin compression for multiple sectors.

factors.name: Fed independence uncertainty (Warsh debut)
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first FOMC (June) faces pressure to balance Trump rate-cut demands against hawkish data. Markets skeptical independence can be maintained; uncertainty adds near-term risk premium.

factors.name: Morgan Stanley 'healthy reset' narrative
factors.summary: Strategist Wilson argues earnings and economic backdrop support stocks; pullback seen as tactical reset. Some Wall Street consensus that Friday drop was overdone and valuations still reasonable on FY earnings.

factors.name: AI capex / hyperscaler demand still intact
factors.summary: NVIDIA-Hyundai AI/robotics deepening partnership; Eli Lilly jumps on weight-loss drug trial success (pharma/biotech spillover). SpaceX IPO momentum and M&A (Intesa/Paschi, Ingredion/Tate & Lyle) show deal appetite.

factors.name: Dollar strength on hawkish Fed bets
factors.summary: USD net long positioning quadrupled; DXY stronger on rate expectations. Weaker EUR/USD (1.1515, two-month lows) hurts multinational tech revenue translation and NDX earnings outlook.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk premium embedded
factors.summary: Iran-Israel escalation (30 missiles fired, petrochemical targets hit) threatens fragile ceasefire and US diplomatic efforts. Trump appeals for pause but conflict durability uncertain; oil/vol premium persists into week.

factors.name: Prior SELL bias (6/10) unconfirmed
factors.summary: Two prior Haiku reads on 6/8 showed SELL6/10 with 'no confirmation triggered.' Monday's -2.73% selloff now validates timing but move is already in progress; late entry risk high.

key_levels.support: 28700–28800 (4h/daily floor near Friday close), 28500 (1% from current)

screening.narrative: NDX is in Phase 3 of a tech-led correction sparked by Friday's hot NFP data (stronger job growth, lower unemployment) that repriced Fed rate-hike odds and compressed long-duration growth multiples. Real yields and nominal yields are rising on inflation fears; oil spiked on Iran-Israel missile exchanges, adding stagflation risk and hawkish Fed repricing. The 2.73% 24h drop reflects broadening semiconductor weakness (SK Hynix, Samsung, ASML spillover) and profit-taking in mega-cap names (NVDA, Broadcom, SoftBank -6%). While Morgan Stanley strategists argue the reset is 'healthy' and earnings growth supports stocks through year-end, the move is already extended and late-entry setup is poor. Fed Chair Warsh's first FOMC looms, adding uncertainty on policy independence vs. Trump pressure.
catalysts_ahead: ECB rate decision Thu 6/12 (25bp hike expected, guidance on future moves critical for EUR/USD and cross-asset yields)
US CPI data this week (key test of inflation trajectory post-NFP; surprise direction critical for NDX rate expectations)
FOMC meeting (Warsh debut; focus on dot plot and guidance vs. Trump rate-cut narrative)
NVIDIA / MSFT / GOOGL earnings updates (any downgrade to hyperscaler capex / AI demand guidance would extend selloff)
Middle East ceasefire developments (Trump-brokered negotiations; escalation = oil shock, stagflation fears)
Bank of Japan hawkish signals (BoJ tightening bets lifting JPY carry unwind risk; USD/JPY vulnerability could spillback to NDX risk-off flow)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidates if: (1) US CPI/PCE surprise cool, reducing hawkish Fed repricing and allowing real yields to fall (bullish for NDX); (2) Iran-Israel ceasefire holds + Trump diplomatic win reduces geopolitical premium and oil deflates; (3) mega-cap earnings (NVDA, MSFT) surprise to upside on hyperscaler cloud capex commitment, triggering short-squeeze rally in Tech; (4) Morgan Stanley narrative gains traction and institutional buying re-enters near $28700 support, reversing Phase 3 into Phase 2 bounce.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended (Phase 3, -2.73% 24h). SELL bias intact but late entry risky — wait for retest of $29200–29400 resistance before shorting, or hold for pullback toward $28700–28800 support if intraday reversal begins. Avoid chasing down; week is catalyst-heavy (ECB Thu, CPI, FOMC), cap conviction at 5 until post-event clarity.

factors.name: Semiconductor/Tech Rebound Post-Selloff
factors.summary: MU +8.79%, INTC +10.78%, ASML, LAM, MARVELL all surging post-Friday crash. S&P 500 inclusion catalysts (MARVELL +10%) and earnings/supply agreement optimism driving sector rotation back into chips.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk-Off Easing (Medium-term)
factors.summary: Iran announced 'end of military operations' and Israel halted strike preparations after Trump/Netanyahu intervention. Oil erased most gains from $98 peak, reducing acute macro shock. Fragile but calming headline risk.

factors.name: Fed Tightening Re-pricing (Macro Headwind)
factors.summary: May jobs beat expectations; Fed funds futures now pricing tightening, not easing (per FXStreet/UOB). Schwab's Martin suggests 'bar to action is getting lower' for Fed hike. Long-duration tech multiples face compression.

factors.name: Core CPI Still Elevated, May Print Pending
factors.summary: TD Securities expects May core CPI 0.23% m/m, 2.8% y/y (still above Fed comfort zone). Real yields remain elevated; critical data point missing from this window but scheduled soon. Hawkish inflation backdrop intact.

factors.name: Real Yields & 10Y Structurally Higher
factors.summary: Friday's jobs beat + geopolitical oil spike pushed yields higher. Long-duration growth stocks (Nasdaq's core holding) extremely sensitive to real rate compression. Hawkish Fed repricing caps multiple expansion despite day's rebound.

factors.name: Mega-Cap AI/Capex Demand Resilience
factors.summary: Amazon-Corning multi-billion fiber optics deal, Corning +4%. Cerebras, ASML, LRCX, LAM all rallying. Hyperscaler capex commitment suggests sustained AI infrastructure demand and earnings support, though guidance not yet updated in this window.

factors.name: Broad Index Divergence & Nasdaq Weakness
factors.summary: News headline: 'NASDAQ bias remains lower' while S&P is neutral-to-balanced. Breadth deteriorating despite rebound attempt; rotation away from mega-cap tech into defensive/cyclical names. Morning premarket rally (+500pts) failed to hold.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Hawkish Fed Expectations
factors.summary: USD mostly lower early session, but underpinned by Fed repricing and risk-off flows (per BBH, ING). Strong dollar pressures NDX international revenue and tech stock multiples. Conflicting intraday moves, but structural headwind.

factors.name: Employment Trends Index Moderation
factors.summary: Conference Board ETI fell to 107.01 May vs 107.88 April (revised), signaling softening labor market ahead. Leading indicator for payroll employment suggests hiring momentum fading despite Friday's beat — forward-looking softness.

factors.name: Sentiment: Morgan Stanley Calls 'Healthy Reset'
factors.summary: Morgan Stanley equity strategist Wilson frames selloff as healthy pullback in bull market; improving earnings and economic backdrop support stocks. Contrarian indicator that institutional bullish backdrop intact despite tactical pain.

key_levels.resistance: 18,300–18,400 (premarket high, Friday open level), 18,650–18,800 (structural resistance pre-selloff)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq staged a partial relief bounce today (+1.12%, ~290pts) after Friday's brutal -1100pt crash on combined geopolitical shock (Iran-Israel missile exchange) and hawkish Fed repricing (strong jobs data, CPI still elevated). Semiconductors and AI infrastructure plays (INTC, MU, ASML, LAM, ASML) led recovery, fueled by supply agreement optimism and mega-cap capex resilience. However, the bounce is PHASE_2 and showing structural weakness: headline 'NASDAQ bias remains lower' despite intraday gains, premarket rally fizzled, employment forward-looking indicators softening, and real yields still elevated under hawkish Fed re-pricing. The geopolitical de-escalation (Iran's announced 'end of ops', Israel pause, Trump mediation) removes acute tail risk but doesn't resolve the macro headwind of higher-for-longer rates crushing long-duration growth multiples. Breadth divergence (S&P more stable, NQ lagging) suggests rotation away from mega-cap NDX names into defensives and cyclicals.
catalysts_ahead: May CPI / Core CPI data release (date TBD, likely week of Jun 9–13) — hot inflation would confirm hawkish Fed, compress NDX multiples further
FOMC meeting / Powell presser (date TBD, June window likely) — guidance on rate hike vs hold critical for long-duration equities
Mega-cap earnings: Apple, Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta, Nvidia earnings in next 4–6 weeks — forward guidance on cloud capex and AI demand crucial
China-Taiwan tensions / chip export controls update — geopolitical wildcard for semiconductor supply
Treasury yield trajectory (10Y, TIPS) — real yield moves drive Nasdaq duration sensitivity; watch for break above recent highs
screening.risk_warning: BUY bias invalidated if: (1) CPI prints hot, confirming elevated inflation and forcing Fed to signal earlier/larger hike; (2) Geopolitical tensions re-escalate (Lebanon strikes, US involvement); (3) Real yields break sharply higher, crushing long-duration NDX multiples; (4) Mega-cap earnings guide down on AI saturation or macro slowdown; (5) Breadth fails to improve and rotation away from Nasdaq accelerates. Monitor RTH close and Tuesday open for conviction test.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is PHASE_2 (rebound underway, premarket high failed). Wait for pullback to 17,900–18,000 support before adding long; if resistance at 18,300–18,400 rejected, odds of deeper retest to 17,700–17,800 rise. Avoid chasing intraday spike; real yield data and CPI pending make conviction entries risky into event risk.
